text:
The Matildas' 18-player squad for the Paris Olympics has been unveiled, with coach Tony Gustavsson hoping a couple of shock decisions will pay off.

Matildas coach Tony Gustavsson has taken a gamble on his Olympics squad, with one promising star missing out while two players carrying injuries make the cut.

The 18-player squad was named on Tuesday morning, more than 12 hours after Australia were farewelled by a rampant home crowd in their last home game before their campaign in Paris.

Vital forward Caitlin Foord has been selected despite carrying a hamstring injury while midfielder Kaitlin Gorry was also picked while dealing with an ankle issue that kept her out of Monday's match.

Both players have just under two months to get back to full fitness before their first match against Germany on July 25 (local time).

Young defender and fan favourite Charlotte Grant was left out of the squad in heartbreaking news for the 22-year-old who has been a mainstay of the senior squad in the last three years and was a part of the Matildas' team at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021.

Veteran goalkeeper Lydia Williams, who is retiring from international football after the Olympics, was also not picked however she has been named as a travelling reserve player along with Grant, Sharn Freier and Courtney Nevin.

The reserves can be selected as a replacement in the team in case of an injury or withdrawal from one of the 18 players named.

Captain Sam Kerr is the biggest name missing from the squad, after she was officially ruled out last month with the striker continuing her recovery from an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury she suffered in January while in a mid-season training camp in Morocco with club side Chelsea FC.

Defender Steph Catley will continue to wear the captain's armband, with Ellie Carpenter and Emily Van Egmond have been named as vice captains.

Aivi Luik, Alex Chidiac and Kyah Simon join Williams, Grant, Kerr and Nevin in being the only players selected in the 2023 Women's World Cup squad to not travel to the Olympics as well.

A large crowd of 76,000 people turned out at Accor Stadium to watch as the Matildas overcame China to win 2-0 in their second friendly in a matter of four days.

After a 1-1 draw in Adelaide on Friday, the match in Sydney was heading for a similar result with scores locked at half-time.

Substitute Clare Wheeler stepped up when she broke the deadlock with a header from a Catley free kick early in the second stanza.

Hayley Raso sealed the result with a tidy finish after she was put through on goal by an incisive Cortnee Vine pass.

Australia plays Germany on July 25 local time (July 26 3am AEST), Zambia on July 28 local time (July 29 3am AEST) and the United States on July 31 local time (August 1 3am AEST).

Full squad: Mackenzie Arnold, Ellie Carpenter, Steph Catley, Kyra Cooney-Cross, Caitlin Foord, Mary Fowler, Katrina Gorry, Michelle Heyman, Clare Hunt, Alanna Kennedy, Teagan Micah, Clare Polkinghorne, Hayley Raso, Kaitlyn Torpey, Emily Van Egmond, Cortnee Vine, Clare Wheeler, Tameka Yallop.

title: Two injured stars named in Matildas squad for Paris Olympics as coach Tony Gustavsson makes shock omission | Sky News Australia
